title: Tulotis asiatica
intro: Tulotis asiatica, also known as Indian tulotis, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tulotis asiatica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Tulotis asiatica is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Ayurvedic formulations such as churnas and ghritas.
taxonomy: Tulotis asiatica belongs to the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of legume plants. This species is classified as a member of the tribe Millettieae, a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Tulotis asiatica is a type of climbing or scrambling vine, which is native to certain parts of Asia.
